verbGlossary.CLOSES ITEMS IN: {source} milestone formally retires open entries in {target} tracking structure upon regulatory acceptance (e.g., certificate of occupancy closes items in risk register)
verbGlossary.CODIFIES: {source} document formalizes {target} countermeasures into an enforceable plan of record (e.g., site safety plan codifies mitigation actions into field protocols)
verbGlossary.CONSTRAINS: {source} dependency restricts the project schedule by blocking downstream work until regulatory approval is obtained (e.g., zoning approval constrains schedule {target} by delaying site mobilization)
verbGlossary.CONSUMES: {source} processing duration eats into available project time reserves (e.g., permit cycle time consumes schedule buffer days)
verbGlossary.CONTROLS: {source} permit establishes enforceable controls that limit {target} to an environmental or site risk (e.g., stormwater permit controls environmental risk through mandated BMPs)
verbGlossary.DEFERS: Temporary regulatory approval postpones full {target} resolution to a later date, leaving residual exposure (e.g., temporary certificate of occupancy defers residual risk until final punch-out)
verbGlossary.DOCUMENTS: {source} filing creates an official record of {target} event for compliance and tracking purposes (e.g., OSHA notification documents safety risk incidents on the jobsite)
verbGlossary.DRIVES: Compliance metric motivates or compels progress on {target} reduction activities (e.g., compliance document completeness drives risk mitigation completion rate)
verbGlossary.ELEVATES: {source} exception raises the assessed probability that {target} will materialize on the project (e.g., variance / special exception elevates likelihood assessment for regulatory challenges)
verbGlossary.ERODES: {source} delay progressively diminishes available schedule flexibility on critical and near-critical paths (e.g., regulatory hold duration erodes schedule float)
verbGlossary.FEEDS: {source} data provides quantitative input to {target} metric calculation (e.g., OSHA notification feeds incident rate TRIR)
verbGlossary.GATES: {source} acts as a mandatory prerequisite that blocks activity start until the associated {target} is formally addressed (e.g., demolition permit gates environmental risk by requiring abatement verification)
verbGlossary.GOVERNS: {source} establishes the regulatory authority and conditions that define the boundaries of {target} category (e.g., building permit governs regulatory and permit risk for the project)
verbGlossary.INFORMS: Inspection or permit metric provides evidence-based input to {target} severity evaluation (e.g., inspection pass rate informs risk score for construction quality)
verbGlossary.MITIGATES: {source} or compliance action reduces the probability or impact of {target} (e.g., environmental permit mitigates environmental risk through mandated controls)
verbGlossary.REDUCES: Independent verification lowers the residual {target} remaining after primary controls are applied (e.g., third-party testing reduces residual risk through independent validation)
verbGlossary.RESOLVES: {source} milestone formally eliminates an open {target} by confirming all conditions have been satisfied (e.g., certificate of occupancy resolves regulatory and permit risk)
verbGlossary.SCREENS FOR: Plan review process examines submissions to identify and flag potential {target}s before construction begins (e.g., building department plan review screens for design risk in submitted drawings)
verbGlossary.SIGNALS: {source} or inspection metric serves as an early warning indicator that {target} is emerging or worsening (e.g., permit status signals schedule risk when approvals stall)
verbGlossary.TRIGGERS WHEN DELAYED: {source} delay activates {target} trigger event that may cascade into schedule or cost impacts (e.g., building permit triggers risk when delayed past planned mobilization date)
verbGlossary.TRIGGERS WHEN FAILED: {source} activates {target} trigger requiring corrective action before work can proceed (e.g., AHJ inspection triggers risk when failed, halting work until re-inspection)
verbGlossary.VERIFIES: {source} confirms that construction execution meets code requirements, validating {target} controls are effective (e.g., special inspection verifies geotechnical risk controls through structural testing)
